search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae frequency urgency dysuria guideline UAE PDF
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng123 urinary urgency frequency initial assessment bladder training
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng123 urine dipstick culture referral haematuria
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine and procedure content was outside documentation scope.
The workflow must not classify or diagnose overactive bladder autonomously.
population_applicability: Partial: NICE NG123 applies to adult women with urinary incontinence or overactive-bladder symptoms, while this broad workflow has no age or sex filter; DHA dysuria has infection and complication qualifiers.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected assessment and discussion fields, not automatic diagnosis, treatment, medicine, procedure, or fixed follow-up.
UAE_applicability: DHA is direct for Dubai telehealth urinary-history and red-flag documentation. NICE assessment and conservative-management pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 PDF and current NICE NG123 recommendations with March 2025 minor updates were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA dysuria issue was identified and NICE NG123 remains current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped dysuria and broad urinary-symptom history without inferring infection.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped fever and flank-pain negatives or warning context without automatic escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urgency, frequency, nocturia, urge-incontinence, pelvic-floor, and clinician-performed examination fields in adult women.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urgency-incontinence or overactive-bladder symptom context, relevant contributing factors, and clinician-performed pelvic-floor assessment in adult women.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinalysis and qualified culture documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped haematuria prompts and clinician-decided referral in qualified contexts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified bladder, urethral, voiding, neurological, haematuria, or recurrent unexplained UTI contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped caffeine, fluid, and bladder-training fields only as clinician-documented discussions or arrangements.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting caffeine or fluid discussion and bladder training only when discussed or arranged by the clinician for qualified adult women.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow does not encode adult female eligibility, incontinence classification, infection status, voiding dysfunction, recurrence, or referral qualifiers.

Urinary-retention negatives, symptom progression, glucose or HbA1c, blood pressure, previous records, generic plan, safety-netting, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, medicine or procedure selection, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
